acceptance_criteria.criterion: skills/implement.md requires specialist template loading before any agent dispatch — hard stop, not advisory
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Lines 30-33 of implement.md: 'Before dispatching any agent for a deliverable, you MUST read and load the specialist template from specialists/{specialist}.md as assigned in plan.json. If the file does not exist, STOP and surface the error. This is a blocking requirement, not guidance.' Uses MUST/STOP/blocking language explicitly contrasted with 'not guidance'.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Dispatching an agent without loading its assigned specialist from specialists/{type}.md is a blocking error
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Same lines 30-33: 'If the file does not exist, STOP and surface the error. This is a blocking requirement, not guidance.' The word 'blocking' appears literally.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: plan.json specialist assignments are validated against existing files in specialists/ before dispatch
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Lines 25-28 of implement.md: 'Before starting implementation, validate that every deliverable's specialist field in plan.json references an existing file in specialists/. Surface any missing specialists as errors and STOP — do not proceed with unresolved specialist assignments.' Upfront validation before any dispatch begins.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Step-level specialist modifiers added to skills/spec.md, skills/implement.md, and skills/review.md
acceptance_criteria.evidence: All three files contain a '### Step-Level Specialist Modifier' subsection: spec.md lines 21-25, implement.md lines 45-49, review.md lines 19-23.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Modifier instructions tell the agent how specialist reasoning applies in that step's context
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Each modifier is step-specific: spec.md emphasizes 'contract completeness, boundary definition, and constraint enumeration'; implement.md emphasizes 'incremental correctness, testability, and adherence to the spec'; review.md emphasizes 'acceptance criteria verification, anti-pattern detection, and quality dimension coverage'. Each maps specialist reasoning to that step's decision space.

dimensions.name: correctness
dimensions.evidence: Specialist loading sequence is correct: (1) upfront validation of all plan.json specialist assignments against specialists/ directory, (2) per-dispatch loading of the specialist template, (3) two consumption paths (solo skill load vs Agent tool prompt parameter). The enforcement is a hard stop at both the batch and per-dispatch level.

dimensions.name: maintainability
dimensions.evidence: Specialist Loading section in implement.md is ~20 lines covering upfront validation, per-dispatch loading, consumption paths, and model_hint resolution. Step-Level Specialist Modifier sections are 4-5 lines each — concise and self-contained. No redundancy between files.

dimensions.name: api_contract
dimensions.evidence: Consistent pattern across all three files: same heading ('### Step-Level Specialist Modifier'), same sentence structure ('When working with a specialist during {step}, emphasize... The specialist's reasoning patterns apply to {step} decisions: ...'), same level of specificity. Specialist loading instructions appear only in implement.md, which is the correct location since spec and review sub-agents are dispatched via Team Planning sections with their own specialist/model_hint resolution.

dimensions.name: completeness
dimensions.evidence: All five ACs are addressed. The specialist loading covers the full lifecycle: validate assignments upfront, load before dispatch, choose consumption path, resolve model_hint. The modifiers cover all three target steps with step-appropriate priorities.

dimensions.name: implementability
dimensions.evidence: Instructions are mechanically followable: check plan.json specialist field exists as a file in specialists/, STOP if not. Load specialist content before Agent tool dispatch. The two consumption paths (solo vs multi-agent) give concrete instructions for each mode. Modifiers name specific priorities per step, not abstract goals.
